title: DNS resolution of container names in rootful custom network fail
body:
### Issue Description

With multiple containers in rootful mode on the same custom network each container cannot access the other one by container name. DNS resolution is not working.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ sudo podman network create testnet
$ sudo podman run -dt --rm --network testnet --name web alpine top
$ sudo podman run -it --rm --network testnet alpine nslookup web
```

### Describe the results you received

```
Server:         10.90.0.1
Address:        10.90.0.1:53

** server can't find web.dns.podman: NXDOMAIN

** server can't find web.dns.podman: NXDOMAIN
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
Server:         10.90.0.1
Address:        10.90.0.1:53

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   web.dns.podman
Address: 10.90.0.2

Non-authoritative answer:
```

comments.body: Do you have something else alreayd running on port 53 on the host? Do you see any aardvark-dns error in the journal.
FWIW, I ran your reproducer verbatim and it worked perfectly here.  Main branch and F40.

> Do you have something else alreayd running on port 53 on the host? Do you see any aardvark-dns error in the journal.

Actually there is a instance of pihole running as a container with host networking.
Can this affect dns resolution in rootful mode? Im my case that works rootless, but not rootful.



> FWIW, I ran your reproducer verbatim and it worked perfectly here. Main branch and F40.

I assume you have nothing running on port 53? That would match what @Luap99 said.

If you already have something running on *:53 that aardvark-dns will fail to bind that port on the bridge ip.

See the other links for work arounds

https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/23169
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23226
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23128

Thanks.
Please let me ask one more question:
Would it help to run the dns container not in host network mode? I think the port would be binded anyway?

yes if you forward the port it will still bind it on the host, the way to work around this is to only specific host ips and not the podman bridge ips (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23128#issuecomment-2199655969)
